title: Go to a Line Number in Code
breadcrumbs: Go to a Line Number in Code
text: Go to a Line Number in Code You can jump to a specific line number in the code editor in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. You need to right-click and click Go To on the popup menu to open the Go To pop-up box. The Go To line number option is restricted to the code of the current object. Note: Only (positive) numeric values are supported. If you enter line numbers bigger than the actual number of lines in the code, it will bring you to the last line. The Go To option works within the current container. Additionally, you can use the default shortcut keys defined for Go To feature in the [userkeys] section of the usys.ini file. The default shortcut keys defined in the usys.ini file is Ctrl+G as defined in the following block. Explain this code [USERKEYS] code.gotoline=Ctrl+G Copy Note: You can change the default key combination defined in the usys.ini file as per your preference. Limitation The Go To option is available only in the Write Script and Layout worksheets. It is not available in More Editors . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
